2026-04-09T05:22:18.4124068Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4124264Z   The key changes were:
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4124758Z   1. Changed `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync()` return type from `IReadOnlyList<OutputItem>` to `IReadOnlyList<Item>`
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4125375Z   2. Added `ResponseContext.GetInputTextAsync()` convenience method
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4125835Z   3. Added `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable<Item>)` public extension
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4126192Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4126397Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4127537Z   4. Added the reverse `ItemConversion.ToItem()` conversion, implemented dual caching in `ResponseContextImpl`, and added internal helper methods while making `GetInputText(this CreateResponse)` internal
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4128520Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4128809Z   Now I should review the README to identify any remaining documentation gaps.

2026-04-09T05:22:18.4135075Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4135242Z   However, I notice:
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4135942Z   1. The "Getting started" section is missing "Authenticate the client" - but this is an ASP.NET Core server-side library, not a client library, so authentication may not apply in the traditional sense.
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4136677Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4136828Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4137691Z   2. Looking more carefully at the README, I see `GetInputTextAsync()` is actually shown in the EchoHandler example, and `GetInputItemsAsync()` is mentioned, so the extension methods are documented through examples rather than explicitly in the Key concepts section.
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4138599Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4139664Z   3. The PR introduced several changes that aren't fully documented in the README: the return type change for `GetInputItemsAsync()` from `IReadOnlyList<OutputItem>` to `IReadOnlyList<Item>`, the new `GetInputTextAsync()` convenience method, the `ItemExtensions.GetInputText()` extension, and the `resolveReferences` parameter.
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4140969Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4144373Z   4. So the documentation gaps are specifically around the `resolveReferences` parameter and the `ItemExtensions.GetInputText()` extension method—both are missing from the README even though they're part of the public API changes. Looking at the samples directory to see if there are any new examples needed for the `GetInputItemsAsync` method with the `resolveReferences` parameter, or demonstrations of the `ItemExtensions.GetInputText` extension in action. The main README sections are all covered, but these specific API features could use sample code to show developers how to use them. I'm seeing that the `resolveReferences` parameter and the new `ItemExtensions.GetInputText` method aren't documented in the README, a... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2253 chars]
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4147045Z 
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4147531Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Res..., ref: main, repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4147988Z 
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4148461Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Res..., ref: main, repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4148909Z 
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4149180Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture. Let me identify the remaining documentation gaps:
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4149674Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4150897Z   1. **`ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable<Item>)`** - This is a new public extension method that allows developers to extract text from any sequence of `Item` objects. The README doesn't document this standalone use case (it's only used internally by `GetInputTextAsync()`). A developer with their own collection of items would benefit from knowing this extension exists.
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4152385Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4153059Z   2. **`resolveReferences` parameter** - Both `GetInputItemsAsync(bool resolveReferences = true)` and `GetInputTextAsync(bool resolveReferences = true)` have this important parameter. 
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4153799Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4154819Z   The README shows basic usage but doesn't explain when you'd set `resolveReferences: false` — like when you want to see item references without resolving them for performance reasons or custom handling.
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4155588Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4156536Z   3. **The `GetInputHistoryAsync` / `GetHistoryAsync` method** - The `ResponseContext` also has `GetHistoryAsync()` which isn't shown in any README example, and the documentation doesn't clarify what distinguishes it from the other methods or when you'd use it.
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4157831Z   
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4159542Z   Looking back at the README, it mentions `ResponseContext` with `GetInputItemsAsync()` and `GetInputTextAsync()`, plus the `ItemExtensions.GetInputText()` helper, but these are significant gaps for the new public APIs added in PR #57974. I should file an issue to track these documentation needs.
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4161183Z 
2026-04-09T05:22:18.4162480Z ◆ The README was partially updated in PR #57974 but has remaining gaps: `ItemExtensions.GetInputText()` standalone use and the `resolveReferences` parameter are public APIs not documented in the README.

Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-09T05:22:18.6169020Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T05:21:01.651143057Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Release History\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Initial release of Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.\n- ASP.NET Core server library implementing the Azure AI Responses API.\n- `ResponseHandler` abstract class for custom response handling with `CreateAsync` returning `IAsyncEnumerable\u003cResponseStreamEvent\u003e`.\n- `TextResponse` convenience class for simple text-only responses with a single delegate.\n- `ResponseEventStream` builder for full control over SSE event generation with automatic `sequenceNumber`, `outputIndex`, `contentIndex`, and `itemId` tracking.\n- `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators for emitting complete output items in one call:\n  - `OutputItemMessage(string text)` — emits a full text message output item (handles all inner SSE events automatically)\n  - `OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable\u003cstring\u003e tokens, CancellationToken)` — streams tokens as `response.output_text.delta` events\n  - `OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` — emits a complete function call output item\n  - `OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` — emits a reasoning output item\n- `ResponseContext` with `GetInputItemsAsync()` (returns `IReadOnlyList\u003cItem\u003e`) and `GetInputTextAsync()` convenience for accessing resolved input items and text content.\n- `IEnumerable\u003cItem\u003e.GetInputText()` extension method for extracting text from any sequence of input items.\n- `CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded()` extension for advanced access to expanded input items as `OutputItem` types.\n- Built-in in-memory response provider and execution tracking.\n- Support for default, streaming, background, and streaming+background response modes.\n- `AgentHostBuilder` convenience methods for zero-config server startup via `ResponsesServer.Run\u003cT\u003e()`.\n- Protocol identity registration with `ServerUserAgentRegistry` during route mapping.\n- `x-agent-response-id` header validation matching the Responses API specification.\n- Conversation ID round-trip support in both synchronous and SSE streaming modes.\n- OpenTelemetry distributed tracing via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}
2026-04-09T05:22:18.6193971Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T05:21:01.67657371Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Azure AI Agent Server Responses library for .NET\n\nAzure.AI.AgentServer.Responses is a .NET library for building ASP.NET Core servers that implement the [Azure AI Responses API][product_doc]. Add the NuGet package, extend one abstract class (`ResponseHandler`), and the library handles routing, streaming (SSE), background execution, cancellation, caching, and response lifecycle management.\n\n[Source code][source] | [Package (NuGet)][nuget] | [REST API reference][rest_api] | [Product documentation][product_doc]\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nInstall the library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/):\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses --prerelease\n```\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n- An [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/)\n- [.NET 8.0](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) or later\n- An ASP.NET Core application\n\n### Configure the server\n\nThe recommended way to start a Responses server is with the built-in one-line API:\n\n```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConfigureServer_Tier1\nResponsesServer.Run\u003cEchoHandler\u003e();\n```\n\nThis starts a Kestrel server with OpenTelemetry, health checks, server user-agent headers, and your handler mapped to the Responses API endpoints. The `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` package is included as a transitive dependency.\n\nAlternatively, register the library services manually in your `Program.cs`:\n\n```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConfigureServer_Manual\nvar builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder();\n\nbuilder.Services.AddResponsesServer();\nbuilder.Services.AddScoped\u003cResponseHandler, EchoHandler\u003e();\n\nvar app = builder.Build();\napp.MapResponsesServer();\napp.Run();\n```\n\n## Key concepts\n\n### ResponseHandler\n\nThe core abstraction you implement. The library calls `CreateAsync` for each incoming request and delivers the returned `IAsyncEnumerable\u003cResponseStreamEvent\u003e` to clients via SSE.\n\n**`TextResponse` — recommended for text-only responses:**\n\n```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler\npublic class EchoHandler : ResponseHandler\n{\n    public override IAsyncEnumerable\u003cResponseStreamEvent\u003e CreateAsync(\n        CreateResponse request,\n        ResponseContext context,\n        CancellationToken cancellationToken)\n    {\n        return new TextResponse(context, request,\n            createText: async ct =\u003e\n            {\n                var input = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken: ct);\n                return $\"Echo: {input}\";\n            });\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**`ResponseEventStream` convenience generators — recommended for multi-output scenarios:**\n\nWhen `TextResponse` is too simple but the full builder API is more than you need, use the convenience generators on `ResponseEventStream`. They handle all inner events (`output_item.added`, content deltas, `output_item.done`) automatically:\n\n```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_Convenience\npublic class EchoHandlerConvenience : ResponseHandler\n{\n    public override async IAsyncEnumerable\u003cResponseStreamEvent\u003e CreateAsync(\n        CreateResponse request,\n        ResponseContext context,\n        [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken)\n    {\n        var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request);\n\n        yield return stream.EmitCreated();\n        yield return stream.EmitInProgress();\n\n        // One call emits all text output events automatically.\n        var input = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]        foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($\"Echo: {input}\"))\n            yield return evt;\n\n        yield return stream.EmitCompleted();\n    }\n}\n```\n\nAvailable convenience generators:\n\n| Method | Description |\n|--------|-------------|\n| `OutputItemMessage(string)` | Emits a complete text message output item |\n| `OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable\u003cstring\u003e, CancellationToken)` | Streams tokens as `response.output_text.delta` events |\n| `OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` | Emits a complete function call output item |\n| `OutputItemRea
